mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment rate and increase in payrolls signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods show negative correlation with the composite labor signal (Rank IC < -0.05) over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and retail sales figures indicate strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods exhibit negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) despite positive demand signals.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high-inflation periods show positive correlation with CPI (Rank IC > 0.1) or exceed 1.5% average gains.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals robust corporate capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods of rising INDPRO show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.05%) despite supply-side strength.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 3% indicates strong macroeconomic momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high-GDP growth periods show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.05%) despite strong demand fundamentals.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers monetary tightening, leading to higher borrowing costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-rate environments show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS (Rank IC > 0.1) or exceed 1.2% average gains.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate paired with stagnant payrolls signals labor market fragility, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods show positive or flat performance (mean > 0.1%) despite labor market contradictions.
